_about: Plain-language summaries + topic tags per publication, keyed by DOI. Picked up by build_html.py and rendered into publications.html. Topics drive the filter buttons; recognised values: ml, llms, review, pharm, thesis. A pub can carry multiple space-separated topics.

10.1080/17460441.2026.2641511.tldr: Why open-source platforms like GitHub matter for reproducibility in computational drug discovery, with practical guidance for researchers new to version control.

10.1080/15376516.2026.2628929.tldr: We use AutoGluon to predict drug–drug interactions, and show that automated model search with molecular fingerprints can match handcrafted pipelines while staying simple to reproduce.

10.1080/17425255.2025.2551724.tldr: Where large language models already help with drug-interaction prediction, where they still hallucinate, and what evaluation gaps need closing before regulators rely on them.

10.1016/j.compbiomed.2025.110156.tldr: A methodological audit of tumour-progression ML papers, showing how weak validation choices inflate apparent performance. Also sketches what a more honest benchmark would look like.

10.1016/j.xphs.2025.104147.tldr: What LLMs (ChemCrow, BioBERT, and friends) currently do for formulation and drug-delivery design, and what would have to be true before they replace manual literature triage.

10.1080/13696998.2024.2315864.tldr: A pragmatic overview of where AI is actually paying off in pharma R&D today, and the economic forces shaping where it gets adopted next.

10.1080/17425255.2023.2294939.tldr: The data, validation, and interpretability gaps that keep ML toxicity models from being adopted by regulators, with concrete suggestions for closing each.

10.1021/acs.jcim.4c00363.tldr: My MSc thesis. A machine-learning ensemble that flags molecules that look like real bioactive hits but are actually colloidal aggregators, deployed as a public web server so any group can pre-screen their compounds for free.

10.1016/j.jsps.2023.01.004.tldr: Head-to-head randomised trial comparing virtual and on-campus pharmacy OSCE exams during COVID-19. Performance was equivalent but examiner experience differed.

10.1016/j.jsps.2023.05.024.tldr: Survey of pharmacy students' experiences with remote learning during COVID-19. Reports what worked, what did not, and what should carry over.

10.18549/pharmpract.2022.3.2713.tldr: Pre-registered protocol for a systematic review of pharmacist-led prescribing interventions, locking inclusion criteria in before any data extraction.

10.1080/0972060x.2022.2072177.tldr: GC-MS characterisation of UAE-grown rosemary essential oil, with major-constituent profile and a comparison to other regional sources.

_no_doi_pubs._note: Pubs without a DOI in dois.json are still topic-tagged via their Scholar pub-key for filter support. Add entries here by matching the pub_key hash that build_html.py computes from the Scholar URL.
